change_summary: detail_evidence expanded 79% (2268→4061): added Evidence Exhibit A / Forensic Zoom graph forensics (BOU+GRACE L1A+L1B, r=0.992, AI=1.152, Dst=−383 nT), INTERMAGNET verification, GRACE orbital-mechanics rebuttal, and explicit internal-contradiction paragraph. detail_verdict_text expanded 750% (159→1351): now explicitly rebuts spatial-heterogeneity escape and adds post-hoc / retrodiction framing linking to Kill-Shot #1 and Part 2b.
our_argument_summary: WIN-012 claims a coupling constant κ = 1.67 nT/μGal derived by dividing BOU 2017 magnetic anomaly (−10.9 nT) by Mohe 1997 gravity anomaly (−6.5 μGal). Our c3 Self-Contradicted verdict rests on: (1) κ's denominator vanishes if WIN-013/014 (null SG results) are taken at face value; (2) numerator and denominator come from two different eclipses at two different locations with no simultaneous co-located measurement; (3) monitor.py validation is a tautological identity check — hardcoded 1.677 compared to hardcoded 1.67; (4) new Evidence Exhibit A 'Forensic Zoom' is shown to be incompatible with GRACE data products (L1A naming, orbital geometry) and contains unreplicable statistics; (5) spatial-heterogeneity rescue is explicitly refuted as unfalsifiable because the dome never specifies which locations see effects.

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt (WIN-010, WIN-011, WIN-012, WIN-013, WIN-014 source statements)
monitor.py 'Domain #7' as described in WIN-011 detail_evidence (not available in this clone's raw-text; inherited from c2 validation)
Evidence Exhibit A graph on dome home page (referenced in review prose)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Per WIN-011 detail_evidence: monitor.py Domain #7 computes κ_obs = 10.9/6.5 = 1.677 nT/μGal, compares to hardcoded KAPPA = 1.67 with ±5% tolerance. No external API fetch; both sides of the equality are constants baked into the source. This matches the c2 code analysis and our aggregated 'hardcoded' count in Part 2b.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Entirely hardcoded. The 'check' is 1.677 ≈ 1.67 (|Δ| = 0.4%, well inside 5% tolerance). κ_obs is computed from the same two scalar constants (−10.9 nT, −6.5 μGal) from which KAPPA was originally derived — a self-referential identity.
data_sources_used: None at validation time (no API calls for WIN-012 in monitor.py)
Cited upstream papers: Wang et al. 2000 Phys. Rev. D (Mohe 1997); INTERMAGNET BOU 2017
Our rebuttal also cites Van Camp et al. 2001 (Membach SG null) and Sun et al. 2010 (China SG network null)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The dome's reconciliation 'Confirms electromagnetic, not gravitational' (raw-text 02-wins.txt, WIN-013 source line) is itself an escape clause — it explicitly concedes that the WIN-013/014 nulls are not gravity signals, which forces us to ask whether the Mohe 6.5 μGal was gravity at all. Once the dome calls the effect 'electromagnetic not gravitational,' the denominator of κ stops being a gravity anomaly, and κ as a 'magnetic-gravity coupling' becomes conceptually incoherent. Our current text captures this logically but does not quote the dome's own phrasing — see recommendation below.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None identified in c3. The monitor.py Domain #7 description continues to be supported by the prior c2 verification and by the cross-consistent WIN-011 detail_evidence. The 'L1A/L1B' critique in detail_evidence is about the dome's home-page Evidence Exhibit A (an HTML graph), not about monitor.py, and has been cross-checked for L1A product-name accuracy.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: WIN-011 detail_evidence states monitor.py Domain #7 hardcodes KAPPA = 1.67 and computes κ_obs = 10.9/6.5 — both the expected and the 'observed' are constants; no fetch call. This matches the c2 tag and is unchanged in c3.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Eclipse magnetic anomalies are well-explained by Chapman 1933 ionospheric Sq currents (used in our WIN-010 and WIN-025 findings). Eclipse gravity anomalies in superior SG measurements are 0 μGal (Van Camp 2001; Sun 2010). The dome's κ relabels a null-gravity + ionospheric-magnetic pair as a new 'mag-gravity coupling'. Standard physics already has a mechanism (no coupling; magnetic = ionospheric) that matches the null-gravity data.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Wang et al. 2000 published the Mohe value in 2000. BOU 2017 INTERMAGNET data and the 2024 9-station paper post-date Mohe. The ratio 10.9/6.5 was computable the day both numbers were published — the dome adopts the arithmetic after the fact and calls it a prediction. The derivation appears in monitor.py encoded as a constant KAPPA = 1.67; there is no pre-registration timestamping this derivation independently of the author.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: κ is a ratio of two empirical measurements. There is no derivation from disc_radius, firmament_height(r), or aetheric parameters that yields 1.67 nT/μGal. The dome presents no mechanism explaining why this particular ratio should be the magnetic-to-gravity scaling; it is stated as observed.

holes_found.description: Our detail_evidence argues κ's denominator vanishes conditionally ('if WIN-013/014 are correct'), but does not quote the dome's OWN reconciliation from raw-text/02-wins.txt (WIN-013 line): 'Confirms electromagnetic, not gravitational.' This is the dome explicitly conceding that the eclipse effect is electromagnetic, not gravitational. Once the dome makes that concession, the Mohe 6.5 μGal cannot logically remain a 'gravity anomaly' in κ's denominator — either Mohe was also electromagnetic-not-gravity (in which case κ isn't a mag-gravity coupling), or Mohe was gravity (contradicting the dome's own 'electromagnetic, not gravitational' framing for the null cases). Our current text is correct logically but rhetorically weaker than it could be; it should pin the dome with its own words.
holes_found.recommendation: Add one sentence near the end of the 'Internal contradiction with WIN-013/014' paragraph: 'The dome's own reconciliation — raw-text/02-wins.txt describes WIN-013/014 as "Confirms electromagnetic, not gravitational" — is self-defeating: if the eclipse effect is electromagnetic rather than gravitational, then the −6.5 μGal denominator in κ is not a gravity anomaly either, and κ as a "magnetic-gravity coupling" is conceptually incoherent.' This turns a conditional logical argument into a direct citation of the dome's concession.

holes_found.description: Our WIN-012 detail_evidence accepts the −6.5 μGal value as given for the purpose of the κ critique, but WIN-011's detail_evidence contains a stronger fact we are not importing: per Wang et al. (2000) the gravity 'valleys' occurred at first and last contact, NOT at eclipse maximum, and the paper's own authors wrote 'no significant anomaly during the very solar eclipse was found.' A gravitational-shielding interpretation would predict the largest anomaly at maximum eclipse (maximum coverage of the disc of the sun). This is the opposite signature from what Wang reported. A dome defender reading only WIN-012 never encounters this timing objection — it lives only in WIN-011. The κ argument would be strengthened by a one-liner: even on the dome's own preferred value, the −6.5 μGal appears at the wrong phase of the eclipse to be a shielding signal.
holes_found.recommendation: Add to detail_evidence, after the 'circular' paragraph: 'Even the −6.5 μGal value itself is suspect on the dome's own terms: Wang et al. (2000) report the gravity "valleys" at first and last contact, not at eclipse maximum, and state "no significant anomaly during the very solar eclipse was found" — opposite to what gravitational shielding would predict (peak at maximum coverage). See WIN-011.' This imports the strongest factual critique of the Mohe datum from WIN-011 without duplicating the full case.

holes_found.description: The new Exhibit A forensic-zoom paragraph is excellent but contains one slightly fragile framing. We say 'GRACE measures inter-satellite K-band range changes, not local gravity in μGal — that unit belongs to terrestrial superconducting gravimeters.' Strictly speaking, μGal is a valid unit for any gravity acceleration anomaly, including retrieved gravity from GRACE (once processed to mascons / spherical harmonics and then expressed as equivalent surface gravity). A careful defender could reply: 'Of course we can express GRACE anomalies in μGal after processing.' The real problem with Exhibit A is not the unit — it is that L1A is PRE-gravity processing (raw accelerometer and ranging data) and cannot already be in μGal. The unit on raw L1A output is not a gravity acceleration at all.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten the sentence to: 'The unit on GRACE Level-1A products is not μGal — L1A is pre-inversion instrument data (accelerations in m/s² from ACC1A, K-band range rates from KBR1A, star-camera quaternions from SCA1A; see JPL PO.DAAC conventions). μGal is a post-processing unit applied to retrieved time-variable gravity at surface points. A plot labeled both "raw L1A" and "μGal" on the y-axis is self-inconsistent — it is labeling raw instrument data with a derived-product unit.' This is more precise and harder to rebut.

holes_found.description: The 'Caveat: disturbed-day baseline' note appears in WIN-010's source-line entry in raw-text/02-wins.txt but does not appear in our WIN-012 treatment of the −10.9 nT value. Our detail_evidence treats the −10.9 nT as an accepted magnitude. A dome defender could note that using BOU 2017 data on a geomagnetically quiet day versus a disturbed day could shift the baseline subtraction, and the WIN-010 raw-text itself concedes this. This is a minor point because WIN-010's verdict is 'Std Model Explains' (ionospheric Sq), not 'Refuted,' but it is worth acknowledging that the numerator is itself not unambiguous.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: add a half-sentence to the 'circular' paragraph noting that the −10.9 nT value carries its own disturbed-day caveat (per raw-text/02-wins.txt WIN-010 source line), so the ratio 10.9/6.5 is not just post-hoc but also sensitive to baseline choices on the numerator side. Do not overweight this — the core critique is the denominator.

holes_found.description: The 'same pattern as the 95.2% accuracy figure' aside at the end of the Exhibit A paragraph is a dangling cross-reference. A reader who has not read the earlier 95.2%-as-static-HTML discussion will not know what 'same pattern' means. Either inline a parenthetical or make the cross-reference explicit.
holes_found.recommendation: Change to: 'The claimed statistics (r = 0.992, AI = 1.152) appear as static HTML with no computation in the repository — the same pattern we document for the dome's headline 95.2% accuracy figure (computed nowhere, displayed everywhere).'

stronger_arguments.description: Pin the dome with its own words: 'Confirms electromagnetic, not gravitational' is a dome-authored phrase from raw-text/02-wins.txt (WIN-013 line). Quoting it forces the choice: either the Mohe 6.5 μGal is electromagnetic (so κ is not a mag-gravity coupling) or it is gravity (contradicting the dome's own null-reconciliation). This turns the internal contradiction from a derived logical result into a direct textual quote.
sources: raw-text/02-wins.txt WIN-013 source line in dome repository

stronger_arguments.description: Timing argument on Mohe: per Wang et al. (2000), the gravity 'valleys' were at first and last contact, not at maximum eclipse. Gravitational shielding would predict the maximum at maximum coverage. This is the opposite signature. It is currently only in WIN-011; promote a one-sentence version into WIN-012.
sources: Wang et al. 2000 Phys. Rev. D — as cited in WIN-011 detail_evidence

stronger_arguments.description: Absence-of-mechanism framing: under dome geometry (disc_radius=20,015 km, firmament_height=9,086 km, aetheric scaling), there is no first-principles derivation showing WHY the magnetic-to-gravity ratio during eclipses should equal 1.67. Our text establishes this tacitly by saying 'No new data is fetched' but does not explicitly note that the dome offers no equation deriving 1.67 from its geometry. Adding a one-liner 'the dome provides no derivation of 1.67 from disc_radius, firmament_height(r), or aetheric parameters' strengthens the 'derives_from_dome: false' tag in prose, not just in the table.

kernel_of_truth.description: A fixed ratio between two physically-coupled observables during eclipses would be scientifically meaningful — fundamental constants of nature do show up as fixed ratios (e.g., α, e/m). The dome's attempt to identify such a ratio (even if executed badly) is not an unreasonable scientific move. Also, the BOU 2017 magnetic anomaly is a real phenomenon with a real magnitude — our review accepts that it was observed, and the dome did pick a real number (−10.9 nT) from real INTERMAGNET data. The Mohe 1997 value was also a real literature report (even if contested). The dome is not inventing the numbers; it is combining them in a way that fails.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: A genuine coupling constant requires (a) both quantities measured in the same event at the same place, (b) a mechanism deriving the ratio from first principles, and (c) replication in independent events. WIN-012 fails all three: the two numbers come from different eclipses at different locations with different instruments; no dome equation derives 1.67 from dome geometry; and subsequent eclipses with far superior gravimeters measured 0.0 μGal — meaning the 'replication' the dome claims via BOU 2017 is magnetic-only and does not re-measure the gravity side of the ratio.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender who has accepted our methodological principles could argue: 'The κ claim is conservative — it is not a prediction that ALL eclipses will show gravity anomalies. It is a ratio computed from the ONE eclipse (Mohe 1997) where a gravity anomaly was detected. The later null results (Membach, China) are not contradictions; they are consistent with the electromagnetic mechanism that the dome already identifies in WIN-013/014. The magnetic anomaly (BOU 2017) exists independently of the gravity measurement; κ tells you, when the gravity anomaly IS detected, what magnitude of magnetic anomaly co-occurs. That one such ratio exists is a datum; your "denominator vanishes" argument only applies if the dome claims gravity anomalies must appear in every eclipse, and it does not.'
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Preempt by adding one sentence clarifying what κ must be to count as a physics constant: 'Even read charitably as a "conditional ratio," κ is not a coupling constant in the physical sense — it is a single retrospective ratio of two empirically independent measurements at two different events. A coupling constant is defined by its stability across independent tests of the same coupled quantities; here there are zero independent tests (the only other eclipse with gravity data, Membach 1999, reports 0.0 μGal, so κ cannot even be computed there).'

cross_win_consistency.notes: Our treatment is internally consistent: WIN-010 'Std Model Explains' (ionospheric) and WIN-025 'Std Model Explains' (ionospheric) align with 'the magnetic anomaly is ionospheric, not a new coupling.' WIN-011 'Not Demonstrated' (Mohe gravity) + WIN-013/014 'Misleading' (nulls as confirmations) align with 'the gravity side of κ is either null or suspect.' WIN-012 'Self-Contradicted' follows directly from these. One minor opportunity: WIN-012 detail_evidence should cross-link WIN-011 (for the Wang timing detail) and WIN-010 (for the disturbed-day caveat on −10.9 nT); currently it references WIN-013/014 but not these. Adding the cross-links would let WIN-012 stand alone as the κ attack without forcing the reader to find the timing detail hidden in WIN-011.

claims_checked: GRACE ground track in 27 min at ~7.5 km/s = ~12,150 km
GRACE-1 altitude in Oct 2003 ≈ 500 km (appropriate for 27-min track figure)
Dst ≈ −383 nT for Oct 30, 2003 Halloween storm (G5)
BOU ΔH range −20.6 to +23.4 nT over a 27-min interval during G5 storm is plausible (mid-latitude observatory during extreme storm)
quantitative_verification.computations: 10.9/6.5 = 1.67692… → |1.677 − 1.67|/1.67 = 0.41%, well under monitor.py's stated 5% tolerance. 27 × 60 × 7.5 = 12,150 km. GRACE-1 altitude at launch (Mar 2002) was ~500 km; in Oct 2003 orbital decay was still minor, so ~475–500 km is correct. Peak Dst ≈ −383 nT for the Halloween 2003 storm is the historical record (29–31 Oct). BOU ΔH is consistent with mid-latitude magnetometer response to G5.

deeper_analysis: Cycle 3 confirms that the c2 'Self-Contradicted' verdict has strengthened with the site rewrite. The two biggest additions — (1) the Exhibit A Forensic Zoom rebuttal and (2) the explicit spatial-heterogeneity-escape rejection in detail_verdict_text — are net positives. The GRACE L1A/L1B physics critique is substantively correct and creates a high bar for any dome rebuttal: GRACE L1A cannot produce a μGal time series at a fixed location, full stop. The remaining vulnerabilities are rhetorical rather than factual: (a) not quoting the dome's own 'electromagnetic, not gravitational' phrase; (b) not importing the Wang-timing-at-contact detail from WIN-011; (c) a slightly imprecise μGal-as-unit argument. None of these change the verdict. All are straightforward text edits. The code_analysis tags remain stable: hardcoded / relabels_standard / post_hoc / ¬derives_from_dome. Advocate-mode defense rates 2/5 — the 'conditional ratio' read is the best charitable defense and it fails on 'no second independent co-located measurement ever exists.' Cross-WIN consistency with WIN-010, 011, 013, 014, 025 is clean; quantitative checks all pass.

citations_verified: GRACE L1A product naming convention (ACC1A, KBR1A, SCA1A) — standard JPL PO.DAAC nomenclature
GRACE orbital altitude ~500 km in 2003 era (GRACE-1 launched Mar 2002)
GRACE orbital velocity ~7.5 km/s (consistent with LEO at ~500 km)
Dst ≈ −383 nT for 30 Oct 2003 Halloween storm (historical)
Van Camp et al. 2001 DOI 10.1023/A:1013940928637 (Membach SG null, 1999 eclipse)
citations_unchecked: Sun et al. 2010 Phys. Earth Planet. Int. (China SG network null) — cited without DOI in our detail_evidence; full citation lives in WIN-014 and has been verified in earlier review cycles, so not re-checked here
JPL D-22027 reference (L1B calibration processing) — referenced by document number without URL; cannot be fetched directly from this sandbox

next_review_trigger: Re-review if detail_evidence changes by >20% again, if verdict changes, if WIN-011/013/014 verdicts change (which would affect the κ internal-contradiction logic), or if the dome site's Evidence Exhibit A graph changes (poller should flag this).
